input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance on the current recommended approach for handling keyboard/soft input in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode. The modern approach in .NET MAUI (especially .NET 10) is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle edge-to-edge/insets properly.


The recommended solution for .NET 10 is to rely on `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which is enabled by default and automatically adjusts the view when the keyboard appears, replacing the deprecated `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` that was based on Android's outdated soft input adjustment mode.
